title: corydalis cava subsp. marschalliana
intro: Corydalis cava subsp. marschalliana is a subspecies of flowering plant in the Corydalis genus, commonly known as the Marschall's corydalis. It is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, which are attributed to the presence of compounds such as tetrahydropalmatine and protopine. The health benefits of Corydalis cava subsp. marschalliana include relief from pain, anxiety, and insomnia, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and improve cognitive function. In herbal preparations, Corydalis cava subsp. marschalliana is often combined with other plants, such as valerian and passionflower, to create teas, tinctures, and capsules that are used to promote relaxation and improve sleep quality.
